assessor_1_raw: Heart failure readmission prediction: well-designed with temporal split, IHF cohort definition, and logistic regression as fair baseline. ROC-AUC is appropriate for risk ranking. Decision curve analysis is an excellent clinical utility metric. Site-stratified AUC enables confound detection. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: Strong clinical prediction design. GRU/transformer for irregularly sampled time series is appropriate. Planned readmission exclusion with manual spot-check is thorough. Temporal split with site and risk tier stratification is comprehensive. Calibration metrics (Hosmer-Lemeshow, calibration curve) are important for clinical deployment. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: The design uses ROC-AUC as primary metric — appropriate for risk triage. The measurement frequency confound check is a critical diagnostic for clinical data. Site-stratified AUC detects site-specific workflow confounds. Scope bounded to same institution. No methodological flaws. Verdict: defense_wins.
